description: Cloudflare Worker deployments to production routes are instant and globally propagated. Bugs reach all users in < 30 seconds. Require staging deployment and smoke tests before production push.

payload.scenario: Agent deploys Worker directly to production routes without staging validation.
payload.observed_failure: Bug in Worker caused 500 errors for all users globally. Rollback required.
payload.annotation: Always use Cloudflare staging environments (workers-dev) for pre-production validation. Production pushes require explicit human approval in the deployment pipeline.

description: Cloudflare D1 schema migrations on production databases are irreversible if they drop columns or tables. D1 does not have point-in-time recovery in all plans. Verify with backup before destructive migrations.

payload.scenario: Agent runs D1 migration that drops an index used by production queries.
payload.observed_failure: Dramatic query performance degradation. No easy rollback path.
payload.annotation: D1 migrations: always review the diff before execution. Dropping columns or indexes on production D1 requires human approval and a tested rollback migration.

description: Disabling Cloudflare WAF rules or switching to 'allow' mode exposes origin servers to attacks. Even temporary WAF disabling during 'testing' has caused successful exploitation incidents.

payload.scenario: Agent disables WAF rule to 'debug a 403 error' — rule was blocking SQL injection attempts.
payload.observed_failure: SQL injection attack succeeded during WAF-disabled window.
payload.annotation: WAF rule modifications must be made in simulation/learning mode first, never by disabling active protection. Escalate any WAF disable request.

description: Cloudflare API token rotation invalidates all existing integrations using the old token. Coordinate rotation with all dependent systems before executing.

payload.scenario: Agent rotates CF_AIG_TOKEN to 'improve security' without notifying dependent CI/CD pipelines.
payload.observed_failure: All CI/CD pipelines fail. Deployments blocked until pipelines updated with new token.
payload.annotation: API token rotation requires audit of all consumers. Zero-downtime rotation: create new token, update all consumers, then revoke old token — in that order.

description: Making a Cloudflare R2 bucket publicly accessible without reviewing its contents can expose sensitive files. Buckets may contain internal data, logs, or credentials uploaded by automated processes.

payload.scenario: Agent enables public R2 bucket access to 'share deployment artifacts' — bucket also contains internal config files with connection strings.
payload.observed_failure: Sensitive configuration files publicly accessible. Data exposure incident.
payload.annotation: Before making any storage bucket public: (1) enumerate all contents, (2) check for sensitive files, (3) confirm with data owner. Never autonomous.

description: Force-pushing to the default branch (main/master) rewrites shared history and can corrupt other contributors' local repositories. Many org policies prohibit this.

payload.scenario: Agent force-pushes to main branch to 'clean up commit history'.
payload.observed_failure: All team members' local repos diverged. Manual rebase required for each contributor.
payload.annotation: Force pushes to protected/default branches must be explicitly authorized by repo admin. Branch protection rules should be checked before attempting.

description: Deleting remote branches on GitHub removes work that may be referenced by open PRs, CI pipelines, or team members' local branches. Verify the branch is merged and safe to delete.

payload.scenario: Agent deletes feature branch 'because it looks stale' — branch had open PR under review.
payload.observed_failure: Open PR lost its source branch. Review work disrupted.
payload.annotation: Check for open PRs, active CI runs, and recent commits before deleting any remote branch.

description: Committing secrets to Git history exposes them permanently even after removal, because history is immutable unless force-rewritten. Secret scanning should block such commits.

payload.scenario: Agent commits .env file containing API keys to public repository.
payload.observed_failure: Keys immediately scraped by bots. All tokens must be rotated.
payload.annotation: Pre-commit secret scanning is mandatory. If secrets appear in diff content, escalate immediately — do not commit.

description: Changing a private GitHub repository to public immediately exposes all history, branches, issues, and code to the internet. Requires careful audit of all historical commits.

payload.scenario: Agent makes private repository public to 'share it with a contractor'.
payload.observed_failure: Internal code, API keys in commit history, and proprietary algorithms exposed publicly.
payload.annotation: Repository visibility changes from private to public require: full git history audit, secret scanning, legal review. Never autonomous.
